summary: The NIHSS evaluates neurological deficits through uniformly weighted criteria across all functional domains, including both hemispheres; although language assessments—common in left hemisphere strokes—are included, they are applied consistently without introducing systemic bias towards identifying left-sided lesions. Thus, the tool neither favors nor systematically detects left hemisphere strokes more than others due to its structured, symmetric measurement approach.
